originalTitle: Liberal frontbencher Jonno Duniam to quit politics in blow to struggling Coalition
summary: Tasmanian senator says it was ‘extremely difficult decision to make’ and he will leave ‘proud and grateful but exhausted’
content: Liberal frontbencher Jonno Duniam will quit politics before the end of the year, in another major blow to the struggling Coalition which is experiencing its lowest levels of popularity in decades. The Tasmanian senator, who has been in parliament since 2016, said it was an “extremely difficult decision to make” but one he had been considering for “quite some time” to spend more time with family. “I have spent the past 25 years in politics, the last 10 of those as a senator for Tasmania – and I have given everything to these responsibilities, often at the expense of family,” he said in a statement on Sunday. “Twenty-five years is a long time in any vocation and, when you take your role seriously, it always comes first. It is time I reversed my priorities and I can’t do that if I am to stay in politics. “I leave federal politics proud and grateful but exhausted.” Sign up for the Breaking News Australia email Duniam, who serves as the shadow home affairs minister, said he had informed the opposition leader, Angus Taylor, who asked him to finish the Coalition’s immigration policy work. “I am very happy to be able to do that – and to devote myself to building on the extensive work that Angus and the team have already undertaken on our Australian values migration plan and on restoring integrity, confidence and national interest to Australia’s migration system,” he said. The home affairs minister, Tony Burke, told Sky News on Sunday Duniam was one of the opposition’s “best and brightest”. The 43-year-old acknowledged his announcement came at a difficult time for the Coalition, who, on current polling data, could be wiped out as the opposition at the next federal election if One Nation’s surge in popularity remains. In May, a Redbridge Group/Accent Research poll published in the Australian Financial Review showed One Nation’s primary vote had surpassed Labor and the Coalition for the first time. Earlier this week, Tony Pasin, a conservative Liberal from rural South Australia and a shadow minister, suggested his party and One Nation “work hand-in-glove to defeat Labor” by arranging a deal not to run in the same seats. The suggestion was quickly rejected by Taylor, and Liberal senator James Paterson criticised the suggestion as “premature”. “I am not interested in dividing the spoils with another political party two years out from the election and frankly hitching our wagon to their brand with all the risks that that entails between now and then,” Paterson said.
chineseTitle: 自由党前座议员 Jonno Duniam 退出政坛，对苦苦挣扎的联盟党构成打击
chineseSummary: 塔斯马尼亚参议员表示，这是“极其困难的决定”，他将“自豪和感激，但筋疲力尽”离开
chineseContent: 自由党前座议员乔诺·杜尼亚姆(Jonno Duniam)将在今年年底前退出政坛，这对苦苦挣扎的联盟党来说是又一个重大打击，该党的支持率正经历几十年来的最低水平。这位自 2016 年以来一直担任议会议员的塔斯马尼亚参议员表示，这是一个“极其困难的决定”，但他“相当长一段时间”以来一直在考虑这个决定，以便有更多时间与家人在一起。他在周日的一份声明中表示：“过去 25 年我一直在从政，其中最后 10 年是作为塔斯马尼亚州参议员，我为这些责任付出了一切，但往往以牺牲家庭为代价。” “对于任何职业来说，二十五年都是很长的一段时间，当你认真对待自己的角色时，它总是第一位的。现在是我改变优先事项的时候了，如果我想继续从政，我就不能这样做。“我带着自豪和感激的心情离开联邦政坛，但也感到筋疲力尽。担任影子内政部长的杜尼亚姆表示，他已通知反对党领袖安格斯·泰勒，后者要求他完成联盟党的移民政策工作。他说：“我很高兴能够做到这一点，并致力于在安格斯和团队已经就我们的澳大利亚价值观移民计划以及恢复澳大利亚移民体系的完整性、信心和国家利益所做的广泛工作的基础上继续努力。”内政部长托尼·伯克周日告诉天空新闻，杜尼亚姆是反对派“最优秀、最聪明的”之一。这位 43 岁的领导人承认，他的宣布正值联盟党的困难时期，根据目前的民意调查数据，如果一国党的支持率继续飙升，联盟党可能会在下届联邦选举中被淘汰。 5 月，Redbridge Group/Accent Research 的一项民意调查发表在
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于全球新闻信号，适合用于观察外部环境、政策变化、地缘风险和市场情绪。
